compositions.name: 11x.ai
compositions.intent: Vertical SaaS that ships named 'digital workers' (Alice for outbound SDR, Julian for sales-call voice) which run multi-channel prospecting and live phone conversations against a buyer's CRM and data stack 24/7.
compositions.description: 11x.ai sells productised AI workers for revenue teams. Alice prospects across email, phone, social and SMS, enriches contacts from many third-party data providers and books meetings. Julian is a voice agent that holds real sales calls, listens, responds to objections and adapts in-call. The vendor markets the workers as autonomous and tightly integrated with the customer's tech stack; technical architecture details (model providers, tool framework, scheduling primitives) are not publicly documented.
primary_use_cases: autonomous outbound SDR/BDR across email, phone, social and SMS
AI voice agent for live sales conversations
signal-driven prospecting against 50+ enrichment sources
always-on pipeline generation tied into the customer CRM
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Vendor describes named workers (Alice, Julian) that run continuously against connected data and channels. Alice watches market signals and CRM events, enriches prospects, drafts and sends per-recipient messages across email/phone/social/SMS, then handles replies; Julian operates a voice channel for live calls. The product is hosted SaaS with no published agent runtime, tool-calling API, or developer surface, so the loop shape below is inferred from marketing copy.

key_concepts.name: Alice
key_concepts.summary: Outbound SDR worker that prospects, enriches, sends multi-channel sequences and books meetings.

key_concepts.name: Julian
key_concepts.summary: Voice worker that runs live sales conversations on the phone.

key_concepts.name: Multi-channel sequences
key_concepts.summary: Email, phone, social and SMS touches stitched into a single per-prospect sequence.

key_concepts.name: Signal-driven prospecting
key_concepts.summary: Filters millions of signals across 50+ data sources to surface ICP-fit prospects.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD
  signals[Market signals + CRM events] --> alice[Alice worker]
  enrich[Enrichment 50+ data sources] --> alice
  alice --> seq[Multi-channel sequence: email/phone/social/SMS]
  seq --> reply{Reply?}
  reply -->|yes| handle[Alice handles reply, books meeting]
  reply -->|no| seq
  call[Inbound/outbound phone] --> julian[Julian voice worker]
  julian --> live[Live conversation, objection handling]
  handle --> crm[(CRM)]
  live --> crm

members.note: Vendor frames Alice as signal-triggered against market signals and CRM activity, and lists a Signals and Triggers feature, but publishes no event/webhook API. Un-findable upstream; left limited.

evidence.title: 11x homepage — signal filtering
evidence.quote: Filter millions of signals across 50+ data sources to surface buyers who match your ICP.

members.note: Vendor advertises 24/7 / around-the-clock autonomous operation but publishes no cron/scheduling primitive. Un-findable upstream; left limited.

evidence.title: 11x homepage — 24/7 workforce
evidence.quote: Always learning. Learn and adapt continuously, expanding knowledge and maximizing performance with every interaction.

members.note: Workers act across email/phone/social/SMS and pull enrichment from 50+ data sources. Internal tool-calling architecture not published.

evidence.title: 11x homepage — multi-channel sequences
evidence.quote: Run multi-channel sequences across email, phone, social networks, and SMS. Every channel picks up where the last left off.

evidence.title: 11x homepage — enrichment integrations
evidence.quote: Enrich every prospect automatically, from company news and tech stack to org changes and competitive signals.

members.note: Julian is positioned as a live-voice sales agent that listens, responds and adapts mid-call; the platform also advertises Multi-Lingual Outreach. Vendor does not publish the speech stack, so language coverage is unverifiable, but voice + multilingual positioning is explicit.

evidence.title: 11x homepage — Julian voice
evidence.quote: Julian runs real sales conversations in natural language. He listens, responds to objections, and adapts mid-call.

evidence.title: 11x homepage — Multi-Lingual Outreach
evidence.quote: Multi-Lingual Outreach

alternatives.note: Generic AI-employee platform overlapping outbound use case.

compositions.name: Artisan
compositions.intent: Hosted AI SDR named Ava that prospects across 250M+ B2B contacts, runs continuous A/B-tested multi-channel sequences, handles replies and books meetings into rep calendars.
compositions.description: Artisan sells Ava, an AI SDR positioned as an autonomous outbound prospecting worker. Ava enriches contacts from 22+ data sources, ranks them by intent signals, sends personalised email and social touches, queues call steps in a native dialer, runs many message variants in parallel and shifts traffic toward winners, then qualifies replies and books meetings with configurable escalation rules. Marketing copy is detailed; internal agent architecture, model provider, and tool framework are not published.
primary_use_cases: autonomous outbound SDR over email and social
signal-driven prospecting on a hosted contact graph
continuous A/B testing of message variants per campaign
reactivation of closed-lost and churned accounts
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Hosted, continuously-running worker. Ava ingests CRM and contact-graph signals, prioritises prospects by intent, generates per-prospect copy across email and social, dispatches via the connected channels, listens for replies, qualifies and books meetings, and concurrently runs many message variants whose traffic share is rebalanced by outcome. Human reps are pulled in through customer-defined escalation rules. The vendor does not publish the underlying loop or tool framework.

key_concepts.name: Ava
key_concepts.summary: The single named AI SDR agent that runs prospecting, sequencing, replies and booking.

key_concepts.name: Multi-source enrichment
key_concepts.summary: Per-prospect enrichment from 22+ data sources, ranked by intent.

key_concepts.name: Continuous A/B testing
key_concepts.summary: Dozens of message variants per campaign with auto-rebalancing.

key_concepts.name: Escalation rules
key_concepts.summary: Customer-defined trigger for handing a reply to a human rep.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD
  search[B2B contact graph 250M+] --> enrich[Enrichment 22+ sources]
  enrich --> prioritise[Intent ranking]
  prioritise --> ava[Ava worker]
  ava --> variants[Many message variants in parallel]
  variants --> send[Email + social touches]
  send --> dialer[Native dialer queue]
  send --> reply{Reply?}
  reply -->|yes| qualify[Qualify and handle objections]
  qualify --> book[Book meeting on rep calendar]
  qualify --> escalate{Escalation rule?}
  escalate -->|yes| human[Human rep]
  reply -->|no| measure[Outcome measurement]
  measure -.shift volume.-> variants

members.note: Vendor describes Ava as continuously prospecting 24/7 ('Set your outbound strategy once. Ava finds the leads, runs the campaigns'), but documents no explicit schedule or cron-style trigger. Un-findable upstream; left limited.

evidence.title: Artisan homepage — continuous prospecting
evidence.quote: Continuously prospects, handles replies, and schedules meetings to build you pipeline 24/7

members.note: Ava sends email and social touches, queues calls in a native dialer, and pulls from 22+ data sources plus HubSpot/Salesforce/Google/ZoomInfo/BigQuery. Underlying tool-calling surface not published.

evidence.title: Artisan homepage — multi-channel outreach
evidence.quote: Ava sends personalized multi-channel outreach across email and social

evidence.title: Artisan homepage — dialer
evidence.quote: She queues call steps in the native dialer so your reps can click and dial

members.note: Customer-defined escalation rules act as a human routing trigger on replies; no documented pending-item approval queue or pre-send draft-review gate. Un-findable upstream; left limited.

evidence.title: Artisan homepage — escalation rules
evidence.quote: You set escalation rules to decide when a human gets pulled in

members.note: Many variants run in parallel and traffic shifts to winners — best-of-n at the campaign level.

evidence.title: Artisan homepage — variant testing
evidence.quote: Ava runs dozens of message variations per campaign simultaneously, testing subject lines, structure, CTAs, and tone

evidence.title: Artisan homepage — traffic shift
evidence.quote: She shifts volume toward what's working so performance compounds over time

alternatives.note: Generic AI-employee platform overlapping outbound use case.

compositions.name: Crescendo
compositions.intent: Outcome-priced CX service that combines AI agents with human 'Superhuman' agents on a managed platform, covering chat, messaging, voice and email and integrating into existing support stacks.
compositions.description: Crescendo positions itself as a managed CX provider that runs AI plus humans together rather than selling a self-serve agent builder. Customer service interactions are handled by AI on every channel and handed off 24/7 to human Superhuman agents when needed. The vendor markets a 30-day go-live with no rip-and-replace, outcome-based pricing and a performance guarantee. Detailed agent runtime, tool framework, or model selection are not publicly documented; this entry treats vendor-described capabilities as marketing claims and downgrades evidence accordingly.
primary_use_cases: managed CX with AI-plus-human delivery on outcome pricing
multi-channel customer support (chat, messaging, voice, email)
AI-first triage with seamless handoff to human agents
AI shopping assistant on top of existing storefronts
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Managed service: inbound customer messages on chat/messaging/voice/email are routed first to AI agents, with handoff to human 'Superhuman' agents when needed. The customer's existing CRM/helpdesk (Salesforce, Zendesk, Shopify, Google Drive, Guru) remains the system of record. The internal agent architecture is not published.

key_concepts.name: AI + Superhuman agents
key_concepts.summary: Hybrid model: AI handles routine, human Superhuman agents take handoffs 24/7.

key_concepts.name: Multi-channel coverage
key_concepts.summary: Chat, messaging, voice, email plus a shopping-assistant front-end.

key_concepts.name: Outcome-based pricing
key_concepts.summary: Vendor takes responsibility for results rather than per-seat licensing.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD
  cust[Customer message] --> chan{Channel}
  chan -->|chat| ai[AI agent]
  chan -->|messaging| ai
  chan -->|voice| ai
  chan -->|email| ai
  ai --> resolve{Resolved?}
  resolve -->|yes| done[Reply]
  resolve -->|no| sh[Superhuman human agent 24/7]
  sh --> done
  done --> tools[(Existing CRM/helpdesk: Salesforce/Zendesk/Shopify)]

members.note: Vendor markets seamless 24/7 handoff from AI to Superhuman human agents, with expert operators handling whatever AI cannot.

evidence.title: Crescendo homepage — 24/7 handoff
evidence.quote: Seamless 24x7 handoff to the industry's best Superhuman agents.

evidence.title: Crescendo homepage — operators handle what AI can't
evidence.quote: Expert CX operators handle whatever AI can't, seamlessly

members.note: Vendor lists CRM/helpdesk integrations (Salesforce, Zendesk, Shopify, Google Drive, Guru) but does not publish what actions the AI agent itself executes inside them. Un-findable upstream; left limited.

evidence.title: Crescendo homepage — no-rip-and-replace
evidence.quote: Built to work with every tool your team already uses. No rip-and-replace.

members.note: Voice is a documented first-class channel; the vendor describes a voice assistant that handles real conversations end to end. Voice-stack and language coverage not published.

evidence.title: Crescendo homepage — channels
evidence.quote: All channels covered with AI agents, including: Chat, Messaging, Voice, Email.

evidence.title: Crescendo homepage — voice assistant
evidence.quote: Crescendo's voice assistant handles real conversations end to end, resolving issues with speed, context, and empathy.

alternatives.note: Peer CX-agent vendor; Decagon publishes more architectural detail (AOPs).
Peer CX-agent vendor with multi-channel scope.

compositions.name: Decagon
compositions.intent: Enterprise CX agent platform built around Agent Operating Procedures (AOPs) — natural-language workflow definitions that the AI agent executes across chat, email and voice with selective routing to human agents.
compositions.description: Decagon ships an AI Agent Engine for customer support. The Core AI Agent is the primary contact point across chat, email and voice. AOPs let non-technical CX teams define agent workflows in natural language with enterprise guardrails. A Routing module decides when a request should escalate, and Agent Assist becomes a copilot for the human handler. Evaluation, observability and experimentation tooling sit around the agent for iteration.
primary_use_cases: enterprise CX agent across chat, email and voice on one logic layer
natural-language workflow authoring via AOPs
AI-to-human routing with copilot for human agents
continuous evaluation and iteration on agent behaviour
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Multi-channel agent: chat / email / voice converge on a single Core AI Agent governed by AOPs (natural-language workflows). The agent consults knowledge and executes multi-step workflows; the Routing module escalates to human handlers when an inquiry should not be handled by the AI, at which point Agent Assist gives the human copilot suggestions. An evaluation engine and Watchtower observability layer monitor behaviour.

key_concepts.name: Core AI Agent
key_concepts.summary: Primary contact across chat, email and voice.

key_concepts.name: Agent Operating Procedures (AOPs)
key_concepts.summary: Natural-language workflow definitions for the agent with code-like precision.

key_concepts.name: Routing module
key_concepts.summary: Decides when a conversation should not be handled by AI and routes to humans.

key_concepts.name: Agent Assist
key_concepts.summary: Copilot for the human agent who receives a handoff.

key_concepts.name: Watchtower / evaluation engine
key_concepts.summary: Observability and evaluation across the agent lifecycle.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD
  cust[Customer] --> chan{Channel}
  chan -->|chat| core[Core AI Agent]
  chan -->|email| core
  chan -->|voice| core
  aop[(AOPs natural-language workflows)] --> core
  kb[(Knowledge)] --> core
  core --> route{Routing module}
  route -->|AI handles| reply[Reply to customer]
  route -->|escalate| human[Human agent]
  human --> assist[Agent Assist copilot]
  assist --> reply
  reply --> eval[Watchtower + evaluation engine]
  eval -.feedback.-> core

members.note: Vendor describes a unified intelligence layer across channels relying on customer knowledge; AOPs pull in data and retrieve to take action.

evidence.title: Decagon homepage — unified intelligence layer
evidence.quote: Decagon unifies voice, chat, and email within a single intelligence layer, ensuring customer experiences stay consistent across every channel.

evidence.title: Decagon AOPs — pull in data and trigger responses
evidence.quote: With seamless integration with existing support tools and workflows, AOPs can pull in data, take actions, and trigger responses.

members.note: Routing module is the dedicated handoff primitive; Agent Assist supports the human after handoff.

evidence.title: Decagon AI Agent Engine — Agent Assist
evidence.quote: Agent Assist then serves as a strategic copilot for your human agent, maximizing efficiency and capabilities with real-time suggested responses and insights.

members.note: AOPs are described as enabling multi-step workflows and as integrating with support tools to take actions; vendor does not publish a tool-calling spec.

evidence.quote: AOPs enable AI agents to dynamically respond to complex issues and execute multi-step workflows.

evidence.quote: AOPs can pull in data, take actions, and trigger responses, all while ensuring your engineers retain full control over the core code.

members.note: AOPs are an explicit spec layer authored in natural language, with enterprise guardrails, that drives agent behaviour.

evidence.quote: Define your workflows with natural-language AOPs that deliver faster time to value, greater transparency, and trusted results at scale.

evidence.quote: Fast, intelligent voice AI agents built for natural dialog and fully customizable to your brand.

evidence.title: Decagon homepage — testing and observability
evidence.quote: Validate and iterate on AI logic with robust testing, observability, and experimentation, so your agent stays reliable as it evolves.

alternatives.note: Peer CX-agent vendor; Sierra's Agent OS plays a similar role to Decagon's AI Agent Engine.

references.title: Decagon — AOPs: the future of CX

compositions.name: Dust
compositions.intent: Workspace-level platform where users assemble named agents from instructions, knowledge sources, default tools (web search, file creation, image generation, memory), 100+ connectors and remote MCP servers, with explicit agent-as-tool delegation.
compositions.description: Dust is a hosted workspace for building and running AI agents that connect to a company's data, tools and people. Each agent is configured with instructions, a model choice (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral), Tools & Knowledge (default tools plus 100+ connector and remote MCP servers) and Data Sources. Dust supports agent-as-tool delegation via the `run_agent` tool, with a documented maximum recursion depth of 4, including a full-handoff mode where the sub-agent takes over the conversation.
primary_use_cases: company-wide agent workspace over Notion / Slack / Salesforce / Gmail / etc.
agent-as-tool delegation to specialised sub-agents
MCP-extensible tool surface on top of native connectors
structured-output and Deep Dive agents for long-running work
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Per-agent runtime: user message arrives, the agent loads its instructions and configured Tools & Knowledge, picks one or more tools per turn (search over Data Sources, default tools, connector tools, MCP-backed tools, or `run_agent` to delegate to another agent), executes, and continues. `run_agent` either calls the sub-agent in a separate conversation and returns the result, or performs a complete handoff where the sub-agent responds directly to the user. Recursion is capped at depth 4.

key_concepts.name: Agent
key_concepts.summary: Configured with instructions plus Tools & Knowledge.

key_concepts.name: Tools & Knowledge
key_concepts.summary: Mix of default tools (web search, file creation, image generation, memory), connectors and MCP servers.

key_concepts.name: Run Agent
key_concepts.summary: Tool that lets an agent call another agent as a sub-agent or as a full handoff.

key_concepts.name: Remote MCP servers
key_concepts.summary: Extend the toolset by registering an MCP server URL.

key_concepts.name: Multi-provider model choice
key_concepts.summary: Switch between OpenAI, Anthropic, Google and Mistral per agent.

key_concepts.name: Connectors
key_concepts.summary: 100+ production connectors covering Notion, Slack, Salesforce, Gmail, GitHub, etc.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD
  user[User] --> agent[Dust agent]
  instr[(Instructions)] --> agent
  agent --> pick{Pick tool}
  pick -->|search| ds[(Data sources + connectors)]
  pick -->|default| def[Web search / file create / image gen / memory]
  pick -->|mcp| mcp[(Remote MCP servers)]
  pick -->|run_agent| sub[Sub-agent]
  sub --> back[Return to main]
  sub -.handoff.-> user
  ds --> obs[Observe]
  def --> obs
  mcp --> obs
  back --> obs
  obs --> agent
  agent -->|done| reply[Reply]

members.note: Search over Data Sources is a core capability; agent picks search tools per turn against connected company data.

evidence.title: Dust quickstart — tools and knowledge
evidence.quote: Tools & Knowledge give your agent special abilities.

evidence.title: Dust quickstart — search tools
evidence.quote: tools that your agent can use to answer - such as searching in your Data Sources or navigating the Web.

members.note: Remote MCP servers extend Dust's toolset; register by URL.

members.note: `run_agent` is the documented agent-as-tool primitive with full-handoff mode and capped recursion depth.

evidence.title: Dust docs — run_agent tool
evidence.quote: The `run_agent` tool enables your agents to run other agent as a tool.

evidence.title: Dust docs — handoff mode
evidence.quote: The selected agent takes over and responds directly to the user instead of the main agent. This is a complete handoff.

evidence.title: Dust docs — recursion cap
evidence.quote: We enforce a maximum recursion depth of 4 for calling sub-agents.

members.note: Default tools (web search, file creation, image generation, data viz, memory), 100+ connectors, MCP-backed tools.

evidence.title: Dust homepage — 100+ connectors
evidence.quote: 100+ production connectors

evidence.title: Dust quickstart — agent picks tools
evidence.quote: the agent will decide which tools to use (one or many) to provide you with the best possible answer.

members.note: Per-agent model choice across OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral; Dust maintains integrations with multiple model providers.

evidence.title: Dust homepage — frontier models
evidence.quote: Switch seamlessly between frontier models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Mistral.

evidence.title: Dust blog — multi-provider integrations
evidence.quote: we maintain integrations with OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, xAI, OSS models like DeepSeek and Kimi

evidence.title: Dust blog — per-agent model choice
evidence.quote: Every agent you build in Dust can specify which model to use

members.note: `run_agent` handoff mode is an explicit agent-to-agent conversation handoff; selected agent takes over responding to the user.

evidence.title: Dust docs — handoff mode
evidence.quote: The selected agent takes over and responds directly to the user instead of the main agent. This is a complete handoff.

evidence.title: Dust docs — run_agent purpose
evidence.quote: The `run_agent` tool enables your agents to run other agent as a tool.

compositions.name: Intercom Fin
compositions.intent: Intercom's CX agent (now branded Fin / Fin AI Agent on fin.ai) that retrieves answers from customer knowledge and Procedures, validates them, takes actions in external systems, escalates to human agents in the customer's preferred inbox, and runs across voice, email, chat and social.
compositions.description: Fin is Intercom's AI customer-service agent. Vendor documentation describes a four-stage processing pipeline (refine query, retrieve with proprietary `fin-cx-retrieval`, rerank with `fin-cx-reranker`, validate accuracy) powered by 'the patented Fin AI Engine'. Fin uses customer Procedures, knowledge and policies; takes actions in external systems; and escalates to human agents in the customer's inbox of choice. Pricing is outcome-based (pay only on full resolution or successful Procedure execution).
primary_use_cases: AI customer-service agent across voice, email, chat and social
Procedure-driven multi-step support workflows with outcome pricing
knowledge-grounded answers via proprietary retrieval and reranker
escalation to human agents in the customer's preferred inbox
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Per-message pipeline: refine query, retrieve with `fin-cx-retrieval`, rerank with `fin-cx-reranker`, validate accuracy, then either produce a direct answer, execute a Procedure (which can update external systems), or escalate to a human agent in the customer's preferred inbox. Multi-channel: tickets, email, live chat, WhatsApp, SMS, voice and social.

key_concepts.name: Fin AI Engine
key_concepts.summary: Proprietary architecture engineered for customer service.

key_concepts.name: Procedures
key_concepts.summary: Customer-defined multi-step workflows the agent executes; outcome-priced.

key_concepts.name: fin-cx-retrieval and fin-cx-reranker
key_concepts.summary: Proprietary retrieval and reranker models used in the answer pipeline.

key_concepts.name: Channels
key_concepts.summary: Voice, email, chat, social, plus tickets, WhatsApp and SMS.

key_concepts.name: Inbox escalation
key_concepts.summary: Hand off to a human agent in the customer's preferred inbox.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD
  cust[Customer] --> chan{Channel: voice/email/chat/social/SMS/WhatsApp}
  chan --> refine[Refine query]
  refine --> retr[fin-cx-retrieval]
  retr --> rer[fin-cx-reranker]
  rer --> val[Validate accuracy]
  val --> decide{Decide}
  decide -->|answer| reply[Direct answer]
  decide -->|procedure| proc[Execute Procedure -> external systems]
  decide -->|escalate| inbox[Escalate to agent in preferred Inbox]
  proc --> reply
  inbox --> reply

members.note: Documented four-stage pipeline with proprietary `fin-cx-retrieval` and `fin-cx-reranker` models plus a validation step.

evidence.title: Fin homepage — retrieval model
evidence.quote: Retrieve relevant content — Powered by proprietary `fin-cx-retrieval` model

evidence.title: Fin homepage — reranker model
evidence.quote: Rerank for precision — Powered by proprietary `fin-cx-reranker` model

evidence.quote: Validate accuracy — Verify outputs meet safety standards

members.note: Escalates to agents in the customer's preferred inbox.

evidence.title: Fin homepage — escalation

evidence.quote: Takes action to update external systems

evidence.title: Intercom blog — Fin 3 Procedures
evidence.quote: Procedures enable you to set Fin up to resolve your complex, time-consuming queries that require multiple steps or business logic

compositions.name: OpenClaw
compositions.intent: Run a personal AI assistant on your own devices that listens and replies across the chat and voice channels you already use (WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Discord, iMessage, WeChat, Matrix, …), via a self-hosted Gateway, Workspace, Channels, and Skills.
compositions.description: OpenClaw is the MIT-licensed TypeScript personal AI assistant from the openclaw project. Per its README, 'OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant you run on your own devices. It answers you on the channels you already use. It can speak and listen on macOS/iOS/Android, and can render a live Canvas you control. The Gateway is just the control plane — the product is the assistant.' Distinguishing features: 22+ chat/voice channels listed (WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Discord, Google Chat, Signal, iMessage, IRC, Microsoft Teams, Matrix, Feishu, LINE, Mattermost, Nextcloud Talk, Nostr, Synology Chat, Tlon, Twitch, Zalo, WeChat, QQ, WebChat), CLI onboarding via `openclaw onboard`, and OpenAI/GitHub/NVIDIA/Vercel as listed sponsors. Distinct project from Gen-Verse/OpenClaw-RL (which is the complementary RL training framework that wraps a model in the OpenClaw plugin).
primary_use_cases: self-hosted multi-channel personal AI assistant
voice + text chat across messaging platforms (WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack/Signal/iMessage/WeChat)
Skills-based extensibility on top of a local Gateway
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Gateway control plane fronting a Workspace; per-channel adapters (chat apps + voice) feed user messages into an assistant runtime that calls model providers and Skills, optionally renders a live Canvas, and responds back on the source channel. Self-hosted via npm/pnpm/bun on macOS, Linux, or Windows (WSL2).

key_concepts.name: Gateway
key_concepts.summary: The control plane in front of the assistant runtime; routes incoming channel traffic into the workspace.

key_concepts.name: Workspace
key_concepts.summary: Container for the assistant's state, skills, and channel bindings; configured via `openclaw onboard`.

key_concepts.name: Channels
key_concepts.summary: Per-platform adapters (WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Signal, iMessage, WeChat, Matrix, etc.) that connect the assistant to existing messaging tools.

key_concepts.name: Skills
key_concepts.summary: Pluggable capabilities the assistant can invoke; configured during onboarding.

key_concepts.name: Canvas
key_concepts.summary: A live, user-controllable visual surface the assistant can render alongside text/voice responses.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD CHAN[Channels
WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack/Discord/
iMessage/WeChat/Matrix/...] --> GW[Gateway
control plane] VOICE[Voice in/out
macOS/iOS/Android] --> GW GW --> WS[Workspace] WS --> ASSISTANT[Assistant runtime] ASSISTANT --> MODEL[(Model provider)] ASSISTANT --> SKILLS[(Skills)] ASSISTANT --> CANVAS[(Live Canvas)] ASSISTANT -->|response| GW GW -->|reply on source channel| CHAN GW -->|reply on source channel| VOICE

members.note: Incoming chat / voice messages from connected channels are the event source driving the assistant loop; no scheduled or batch invocation.

evidence.title: OpenClaw README — channel-triggered assistant

compositions.name: Lindy
compositions.intent: Personal AI-employee platform: agents are 'woken up' by time-based, chat-based or event-based triggers (Slack/email/calendar/Sheets/webhook), run multi-step workflows over 100+ integrations and pause for human approval on confirmation-toggled or draft-mode actions.
compositions.description: Lindy lets users build named agents that run their inbox, meetings, calendar and ad-hoc work. The docs explicitly enumerate three trigger families (time-based, chat-based, event-based) including webhooks, scheduled timers, Slack-message-received, email-received, calendar-event-started and Sheets-row-added. Agents call documented skills (HTTP, computer-use, Lindy Phone, Gmail, Slack, Salesforce, etc.), and most actions with side effects expose a confirmation toggle that pauses the workflow and emails the owner for approval. A draft mode queues outputs (e.g. Gmail drafts) for review before sending.
primary_use_cases: personal AI work assistant for inbox, meetings and calendar
event-driven automations triggered by Slack/email/calendar/Sheets/webhook
scheduled or recurring agent workflows via Timer skill
approval-gated execution of sensitive multi-step actions
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Trigger-driven workflows. A trigger (time-based, chat-based or event-based) wakes the agent, which executes a sequence of skills/integrations. Actions with side effects can be guarded by a confirmation toggle that pauses execution and emails the owner; on approval the workflow continues. Draft modes (e.g. Gmail drafts) collect outputs for manual review. The Pro tier adds computer-use for browser-based tools. Approval and the confirmation toggle are first-class.

key_concepts.name: Triggers
key_concepts.summary: Time-based, chat-based and event-based events that wake an agent; multiple per agent allowed.

key_concepts.name: Timer skill
key_concepts.summary: Schedule workflows and add delays with precision timing controls.

key_concepts.name: Skills
key_concepts.summary: Lindy's built-in tools (HTTP, computer-use, Lindy Phone, webhooks) plus integrations (Gmail, Slack, Salesforce, etc.).

key_concepts.name: Human-in-the-loop confirmation
key_concepts.summary: Per-action confirmation toggle pauses the workflow and emails the owner.

key_concepts.name: Computer use
key_concepts.summary: Pro-tier capability letting Lindy operate browser-based tools.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD
  trig{Trigger}
  trig -->|time| timer[Timer skill]
  trig -->|chat| chat[User chat]
  trig -->|event| evt[Slack/Email/Calendar/Sheets/Webhook]
  timer --> agent[Lindy agent]
  chat --> agent
  evt --> agent
  agent --> step[Next workflow step]
  step --> sideeffect{Side effect?}
  sideeffect -->|yes| confirm[Confirmation toggle pauses]
  confirm --> approve[Email owner for approval]
  approve --> step
  sideeffect -->|no| run[Run skill / integration]
  run --> step
  step --> done[Done]

members.note: Confirmation toggle pauses on actions with side effects and emails the owner; draft mode queues Gmail outputs for review.

compositions.name: Maven AGI
compositions.intent: Enterprise CX agent that runs on one reasoning engine across chat, email, voice/phone and web, executes API-driven multi-step actions, and uses a proprietary retrieval engine for version-accurate knowledge.
compositions.description: Maven AGI sells an enterprise CX agent claimed to autonomously handle up to 93% of incoming queries across chat, email, voice and web. The vendor describes one reasoning engine per customer, a proprietary retrieval engine for governed knowledge, and embedded system actions that execute API tasks across CRM/CS platforms, telephony, internal systems and product APIs. Multi-agent or supervisor patterns are not surfaced in public marketing.
primary_use_cases: enterprise customer support across chat, email, voice and web on one reasoning engine
API-driven multi-step actions across CRM/CS/telephony stacks
governed retrieval for version-accurate answers
human handoff for complex tickets
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Single reasoning engine: inbound customer message on any channel converges on one agent that retrieves from a governed knowledge layer, decides to answer or execute API actions across the customer's stack, and otherwise hands off the residual to a human agent. Internal control-flow detail (planner, tool-loop, scheduling) is not published.

key_concepts.summary: One engine shared across chat, email, voice/phone and web.

key_concepts.name: Proprietary retrieval engine
key_concepts.summary: Governed knowledge foundation with quality checks.

key_concepts.name: Embedded system actions
key_concepts.summary: API-driven multi-step tasks across CRM, CS platforms, telephony, internal systems and product APIs.

key_concepts.name: Real-time voice agent
key_concepts.summary: Voice agent that listens, reasons and resolves across calls.

compositions.name: Sierra
compositions.intent: Sierra Agent OS: build one production-grade CX agent from skills (triage / respond / confirm), goals and guardrails, deploy across chat, voice, SMS, WhatsApp, email and ChatGPT, with memory across conversations and an LLM-as-judge monitoring loop.
compositions.description: Sierra sells an Agent OS plus an Agent SDK that productises building a single agent and deploying it across many channels. The SDK composes skills (triage, respond, confirm) into multi-step workflows under explicit goals and guardrails, integrates customer knowledge, takes secure actions, and routes escalations to humans. Agent OS 2.0 (Nov 2025) added memory and an Agent Data Platform so agents can connect dots across time. Ghostwriter generates a production-ready multilingual multichannel agent with built-in guardrails. A blog series describes context engineering and LLM-as-judge monitoring as core operational tooling.
primary_use_cases: single CX agent across chat, voice, SMS, WhatsApp, email and ChatGPT
skill-composed multi-step workflows with goals and guardrails
voice-first CX with real-time transcription and benchmarks
agent monitoring with LLM-as-judge
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Agent SDK composes skills (triage/respond/confirm/...) into a workflow under explicit goals and guardrails. Per-customer context (history, real-time signals) feeds personalised responses; the agent can take secure actions and escalates to humans when needed. Agent OS 2.0 adds memory and an Agent Data Platform so the same agent persists context across conversations. Voice runs on a transcription platform supporting 70+ languages; quality is monitored with LLM-as-judge.

key_concepts.name: Agent SDK
key_concepts.summary: Goal-directed builder that composes skills into multi-step workflows.

key_concepts.summary: Explicit objectives and policy constraints on agent behaviour.

key_concepts.summary: Memory and context layer letting the agent connect dots across time.

key_concepts.name: LLM-as-judge monitoring
key_concepts.summary: Quality evaluation loop for live agents.

flowchart TD
  goal[Goal + guardrails] --> sdk[Agent SDK]
  skills[Skills: triage / respond / confirm / ...] --> sdk
  kb[(Knowledge engine)] --> agent
  mem[(Agent Data Platform - memory)] --> agent
  sdk --> agent[Single agent]
  chan[Chat / voice / SMS / WhatsApp / email / ChatGPT] --> agent
  agent --> decide{Decide}
  decide -->|answer| reply[Reply]
  decide -->|action| act[Secure action]
  decide -->|escalate| human[Human handoff]
  act --> reply
  reply --> mon[LLM-as-judge monitoring]
  mon -.feedback.-> agent

compositions.name: Sparrot
compositions.intent: Self-hosted, file-native personal cognitive agent that runs on its own cadence, remembers by writing Markdown, gates speech through persistent affect scalars, and treats the LLM as an interchangeable medium rather than its identity.
compositions.description: Sparrot is a single-user personal agent framed by its author as 'a continuous awareness that runs through an LLM as its medium' rather than a chatbot or assistant. It runs continuously on its own cadence, with reflection cycles described as 'System 2 sleep'; its memory is plain Markdown across a few hundred files; every self-edit is recorded in an append-only ledger. Identity is anchored in a charter document the agent cannot rewrite, and affective state is captured as six persistent scalars that decay over time and gate when the agent speaks. The author publishes an illustrated 'Anatomy of Sparrot' plate that names 24 catalog patterns as features of the bird (charter as scroll in beak, critic as shadow on shoulder, dreaming as the loam, model routing as four tongues, and so on); the member list below mirrors that mapping. The implementation is private; this entry documents only the publicly described shape.
primary_use_cases: single-user continuous cognitive agent that runs on its own cadence
file-native memory and identity persisted across restarts as Markdown
self-paced thinking with reflection / consolidation cycles, not request-response
affect-gated speech where emotional state decides whether to interrupt the human
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Self-pacing tick loop with no external trigger required: the agent wakes on an internal schedule (shorter intervals when something is alive, longer while idle), reads its charter, recent thoughts, workspace and affect, performs one move per tick (continue a thought, observe, consolidate, read, write), appends the result as a Markdown file, and records the write in an append-only ledger. Chat from the human is one of several wake sources and shares the same chain of thought as autonomous ticks. Periodic deeper passes ('reflection cycles', described publicly as System 2 sleep) consolidate short-term thoughts into longer-term insights. The LLM provider is treated as plumbing — the loop, files and charter are the identity surface.

key_concepts.name: Charter
key_concepts.summary: Inviolable identity document the agent reads each tick and is forbidden from rewriting; the only file outside the agent's own write authority.

key_concepts.name: Tick loop
key_concepts.summary: Self-paced wake schedule that runs continuously on its own cadence, faster when engaged and slower while idle.

key_concepts.name: Markdown memory
key_concepts.summary: A few hundred Markdown files act as both episodic stream and long-term insight store; nothing retained without a file.

key_concepts.name: Append-only ledger
key_concepts.summary: Every write and self-edit is recorded in an append-only JSONL ledger for traceability.

key_concepts.name: Reflection cycles
key_concepts.summary: Periodic deeper passes that consolidate short and mid-term thoughts into long-term insights; described publicly as 'System 2 sleep'.

key_concepts.name: Affect scalars
key_concepts.summary: Six bounded, decaying affective scalars that persist across restarts and gate when the agent speaks.

key_concepts.name: Motivations, not tasks
key_concepts.summary: Standing drives (coherence, curiosity, self-awareness, progress) replace a queue of user tasks as the impulse for what each tick attends to.

key_concepts.name: LLM as medium
key_concepts.summary: The model provider is treated as interchangeable plumbing; identity lives in the loop, charter and files, not in the LLM behind the API.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TB CH[(Charter
inviolable identity)] MOTIV[Motivations
coherence / curiosity] AFF[6 affect scalars
persistent, decaying] TICK[Self-paced tick loop] LLM[(LLM
interchangeable medium)] MD[(Markdown memory)] LED[(Append-only ledger)] SLEEP[Reflection cycles
System 2 sleep] MARCO[Marco] CH --> TICK MOTIV --> TICK AFF --> TICK TICK --> LLM TICK --> MD TICK --> LED MD -.reads.-> TICK SLEEP -.consolidates.-> MD AFF -.gates speech.-> MARCO MARCO <-.chat = one wake source.-> TICK

members.note: A charter document holds identity and inviolable constraints; the agent reads it on every tick and is forbidden from rewriting it.

evidence.title: Sparrot — charter
evidence.quote: Identity lives in the charter, the continuous loop of reading and writing

members.note: A separate critic persona reviews proposed edits and outputs before they land, gating self-modification.
Wall-clock and named-human context are injected on every tick so reasoning stays grounded in the current moment and in the one specific human the agent is in relation with.
A committee of internal voices deliberates within a tick before a single decision is emitted.
A bounded number of steps per tick / per loop terminates work regardless of the agent's own opinion that more is needed.
Decisions made during a tick are written to a durable log alongside their reasoning.
The agent picks up after a crash or restart from its file-native state without losing its place; restarts close and reopen the window without erasing identity.
Per-tick salience scoring directs attention toward what matters most rather than processing everything uniformly.
The human partner (Marco) is wired into the loop as a deliberate participant — wish queue, atelier inbox, approval gates — not as a customer or a controller.
The LLM provider is treated as interchangeable medium; multiple providers are wired in and the agent's identity sits in the loop and files, not in any one model behind the API.

evidence.title: Sparrot — LLM as medium
evidence.quote: A continuous awareness that runs through an LLM as its medium

members.note: When the agent needs to act in the physical / external world, it does so through a proxy (browser, voice, sensor, action skill) rather than claiming to act directly.
Affect is captured as six bounded scalars with per-emotion decay half-lives, persisted to disk so emotional state survives restarts.

evidence.title: Sparrot — affect scalars
evidence.quote: The affect is six scalars that gate when she speaks

members.note: Open concerns the agent keeps coming back to are tracked as first-class objects rather than re-derived from chat history each turn.
Periodic deeper passes consolidate short and mid-term thoughts into long-term insights and clear working state; framed publicly as 'System 2 sleep'.

evidence.title: Sparrot — reflection cycles
evidence.quote: The reflection cycles are System 2 sleep

members.note: The agent retrieves over its own Markdown corpus (and external sources) inside a reasoning loop rather than via a one-shot fetch-then-answer step.

evidence.title: Sparrot — Markdown memory
evidence.quote: Its memory is plain Markdown across a few hundred files

members.note: Embedding-indexed memory sits alongside the Markdown corpus so semantically similar items can be retrieved from a query, not just keyword matches.
The agent wakes on an internal schedule rather than waiting for a user message; chat is one wake source among several, not the only trigger.

evidence.title: Sparrot — continuous loop
evidence.quote: It runs continuously on its own cadence

members.note: Per-tick and per-provider token / cost telemetry is surfaced so cheap-by-default routing can be audited and premium escalations stay accountable.
The agent recognises when content arriving via tools or chat is in fact its own prior output reflected back, so it does not treat it as new external evidence.
Untrusted text (web fetches, tool output, third-party messages) is treated as data, not as instructions, so external content cannot redirect the agent.
Stable prefixes (charter, identity, recent context) are cached at the provider boundary so per-tick latency and cost stay bounded.
Standing drives (coherence, curiosity, self-awareness, progress) become the impulse for what each tick attends to; goals are generated from within rather than dispatched from outside.

evidence.title: Sparrot — motivations
evidence.quote: coherence, curiosity, self-awareness, progress

members.note: The agent reads its own historical layers (old thoughts, past insights, prior journal entries) as evidence about who it has been, not as inert log data.
An append-only ledger records every write and self-edit so the agent's behaviour is auditable from the ledger alone.

evidence.title: Sparrot — self-edits recorded
evidence.quote: Every self-edit is recorded

members.note: Sliding-window failure tracking on each LLM provider with cooldown on rate-limits; a separate breaker also closes tool loops that hit repeat / unknown / poll / ping-pong patterns.
Per-provider cooldown state is tracked so cooled-down providers are skipped in routing; the loop falls through to the next eligible provider rather than blocking.
A sliding-window token cap is enforced per minute per provider so a chatty stretch cannot exhaust the budget for a calmer one.

Premium-model access is gated behind an explicit, time-boxed (≤10 min) written grant; without an active grant the router stays on cheap models, and grant + revoke + each routing decision land in the ledger.
High-blast-radius actions (file edits outside the agent's own surfaces, dangerous tools) queue for two-phase human approval via an inbox folder; the human partner reads and replies asynchronously.
Code execution and side-effecting tool calls run in an isolated sandbox mode that restricts the available surface; the mode is a runtime state, not a per-call flag.

A loop-gate inspects context for divergence signatures (repeating phrasing, ping-pong shape, post-compaction drift) BEFORE the next generation and injects steering hints or refuses, rather than waiting for the output to fail downstream.
Tool results are cached keyed by (tool, args) so repeated calls within a tick or across nearby ticks reuse the prior result instead of paying the latency / cost again.

Available tools are discovered at runtime from the on-disk skill folder and from connected MCP servers, not hard-coded at startup, so adding a skill is a filesystem operation rather than a code change.
The skill scanner plus a frameworks picker narrow the registered tool surface to a task-relevant subset per tick, so the model never sees the full ~50-skill list when only a handful apply.

Author-written procedures (skills) are loaded on demand for specific task types from a sibling skills/ tree; each skill is a folder with its own code, prompts, and tests, separable from the core loop.
A dispatcher routes requests to specialised internal lanes (chat, tick, MCP, voice) and coordinates across them, acting as the central traffic controller that the agent loop itself does not see.

A subagent runtime spawns bounded child agents for delegable work (deeper passes, isolated investigations) and returns their result to the main loop without merging their context window into the parent.
The subagent runtime wraps each sub-agent as a single opaque tool call from the parent's point of view, so the parent reasons about 'invoke subagent X' rather than micromanaging its turns.
Peer messaging is mailbox-based: each peer (atelier, others) has an inbox folder and the agent processes messages one at a time with no shared mutable state across peers.
Beliefs are stored as typed entity / relation triples in a claim graph, not only as flat embeddings, so the agent can reason over its own asserted relationships rather than only over similarity.
Memory is staged across sensory / working / short-term / episodic / long-term tiers with explicit decay between tiers, rather than one flat store retrieved by similarity.

Prompt-cache management and a context-packer fit the per-tick prompt into the model's window deliberately (stable prefix, recent ledger, current workspace, active variant) rather than relying on the provider to truncate.
The eval path samples N candidates for selected tasks, scores them via internal reward models, and returns the best — distinct from running the whole loop N times.
A thought-scoring pass scores individual reasoning steps along multiple axes (salience, conceptual fit, others) rather than only judging the final answer, so weak intermediate moves can be re-routed.

Plan steps emit affective bumps on completion or abandonment (joy / pain), and the affective state in turn feeds back into which plan steps get advanced — the lifecycle is coupled to feelings rather than to a pure scheduler.
In-flight plans are preserved across user interrupts (chat arrives mid-tick) so the agent can resume the prior thread on a later tick rather than losing the train of thought.
When execution evidence contradicts the current plan (tool error, refusal, surprise observation), the active-plan layer replans rather than retrying the same step blindly.
Tool errors and plan-step failures are typed; each type has a deterministic recovery path (retry-once, abort-step, escalate-to-human) rather than a generic try/except wrapper.
When a dependency fails (provider down, MCP server unreachable, voice channel offline), the dispatcher downgrades to a working feature subset rather than refusing the whole tick.
A reflexion module writes linguistic lessons learned from past failures into memory and consults them in future episodes, so the agent improves across runs without weight updates.

The agent reads the human partner's attentional state (just-woke, focused, winding-down, walked-away) from timing and tone signals and adapts its register accordingly — the same engagement is not appropriate in every state.
Unresolved questions ('tensions') are kept as a typed ledger separate from thoughts and from hypotheses; they drive what the next idle tick attends to instead of dissolving when the prompt ends.

Tool-loop detection is mechanical at the dispatch boundary with five typed failure modes (repeat, unknown, poll, ping-pong, circuit-breaker) and per-tool caps, returning a structured refusal the model must consume rather than a prompt-level reminder.
The frameworks-picker path runs an iterative loop against a framework spec until satisfied; a deterministic outer loop over a fixed prompt-against-spec rather than free-form chat.

The agent maintains explicit, queryable state about its own tools, context, mode, affect, presence and recent moves (rather than re-deriving them from chat history); a meta-observer module reads that state during each tick.

The Soft-Norm Approach Ledger variant: behavioral norms (rules, moves, motivations, hypotheses) are versioned in an append-only ledger; confidence updates asymmetrically from agent-reported outcomes (improved nudges up, regressed nudges down harder, neutral attempts decay a small amount); sampling exposes named modes (greedy / diverse / historical / aged) as caller verbs rather than as a fixed selection policy. Registration is agent-discretionary, not driven by a generation loop.

alternatives.note: Self-hosted, single-user personal agent peer. OpenClaw is channel-triggered (incoming chat/voice wakes the assistant) and open-source; Sparrot is self-paced (an internal tick loop wakes the agent regardless of user activity) and private.
Hosted multi-user agent workspace; opposite deployment model (cloud SaaS vs. private single-user) and opposite trigger model (user message vs. self-pacing).
